skill.name: global-error-handling
skill.description: Implement user-friendly error handling with specific exception types, centralized handling, graceful degradation, retry strategies, and proper resource cleanup.
skill.summary: Guide consistent error handling across controllers, services, API endpoints, and external integrations.
skill.author: DevanB

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported three path traversal hits at SKILL.md line 28 and two weak cryptography hits at lines 3 and 8. The path traversal hits are a real contextual concern because the skill points to a parent-directory markdown file, while the weak cryptography hits are false positives in prose. No malicious intent, command execution, network access, or secret exfiltration evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: Parent Directory Documentation Reference
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md line 28 links to ../../../agent-os/standards/global/error-handling.md. This is not executable code, but it can direct an assistant to read outside the packaged skill directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory path is explicit and appears three times in static analysis for the same line. Risk is moderate because the target is a hardcoded markdown reference with no user input, commands, network calls, or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md lines 3 and 8 for weak cryptography, but those lines are natural-language descriptions. No evidence of cryptographic algorithms or crypto API usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations contain prose about error handling and coding standards. The scanner likely matched a substring such as text inside 'description' or 'standards', not real crypto behavior.

content.value_statement: Error handling is often scattered, inconsistent, and too technical for users. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward centralized, user-safe, and resilient error handling.
actual_capabilities: Guides error handling for controllers, services, and API endpoints.
Promotes specific exception types for clear failure scenarios.
Encourages user-friendly error messages without sensitive technical details.
Supports fail-fast validation and precondition checks.
Covers graceful degradation, retry strategies, and cleanup in finally blocks.
Recommends centralized handling instead of scattered try-catch blocks.
limitations: Provides guidance only and does not include executable helper code.
References an external relative markdown file that may be missing outside the source repository.
Does not define framework-specific middleware, decorators, or exception classes.
Requires project-specific logging, monitoring, and retry policy decisions.

use_cases.title: Harden API Endpoint Failures
use_cases.description: Apply consistent responses, safe logging, and resource cleanup around API handlers.

use_cases.title: Design Service Exception Boundaries
use_cases.description: Create clear exception types and decide where failures should be caught or rethrown.

use_cases.title: Improve User-Facing Error Messages
use_cases.description: Replace technical errors with helpful messages that do not expose implementation details.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Error Handling
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single handler or function.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-error-handling skill to improve this function's error handling. Keep user messages clear and avoid exposing technical details.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Scattered Catch Blocks
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate cleanup of repeated error handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-error-handling skill to refactor these repeated try-catch blocks into a clearer boundary-based error handling approach.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Resilient External Calls
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced handling for third-party service failures.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-error-handling skill to design retries, graceful degradation, and permanent failure handling for this external service integration.

prompt_templates.title: Review Error Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across controllers, services, and APIs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-error-handling skill to review this error handling architecture. Identify boundary mistakes, unsafe logging, missing cleanup, and inconsistent exception types.

output_examples.input: Review an API controller with broad catch-all errors.
output: Specific recommendations for validation, exception mapping, and user-safe messages.
Notes about logging sensitive details only in protected server logs.

output_examples.input: Design error handling for a payment provider outage.
output: A retry and fallback strategy separated by transient and permanent failures.
A user message plan that avoids exposing provider internals.
Cleanup and monitoring recommendations for failed attempts.

best_practices: Handle errors at clear boundaries such as controllers, API layers, and integration adapters.
Use specific exception types so failures can be logged, retried, or shown correctly.
Protect sensitive details in user messages while keeping useful diagnostic logs.
anti_patterns: Catching every exception at low-level functions without adding useful context.
Returning raw stack traces, database errors, or provider details to users.
Retrying permanent failures or retrying transient failures without backoff.

faq.question: Does this skill add executable error handling code?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance that the assistant can apply to your codebase.

faq.question: Can it help with API error responses?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers controller and API boundaries, user-safe messages, and centralized response handling.

faq.question: Does it support retry strategy design?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends handling transient and permanent failures differently, including exponential backoff for external services.

faq.question: Will it prevent sensitive data leaks?
faq.answer: It guides the assistant to avoid exposing technical details to users and to log carefully.

faq.question: Is it tied to one programming language?
faq.answer: No. The guidance is general and must be adapted to the project's framework and language.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill contains a parent-directory documentation link. It is not malicious, but it can point outside the packaged skill directory.
